rationale: Khan and Dhar (2006) is not just listed in background; it is explicitly summarized and used to derive testable predictions about reduced guilt and greater indulgence after donation. The citing authors design an experiment around this prior result (including a similar two-task setup) to test their process account for promotion choice. Although the 2011 paper's main contribution is about promotion framing, this cited work meaningfully informs both conceptual framing and empirical design, so the citation is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar's licensing finding (prior altruistic act -> later indulgence) to motivate and operationalize its donation manipulation in Experiment 5. It applies that mechanism to a new outcome domain, showing how guilt reduction affects preference for price discount versus bonus pack on vice versus virtue foods.
